summary: utils-mf ships a 57MB Debian archive as its main entry; no reachable code path
details: The package declares `main: "system.img"` and `index.js` does `require('./system.img')`. `system.img` is a 57MB Debian `ar` archive (containing debian-binary, control.tar.xz, data.tar.xz, repeated three times) wrapped in what appears to be an attempt at a JavaScript block comment (file begins `/**!<arch>`). The wrapping is broken — the embedded binary contains literal `*/` byte sequences, so Node's CJS loader will hit a SyntaxError on import rather than executing anything. There are no lifecycle scripts (`scripts` field is absent), no network calls, no credential reads, and the embedded `.deb` is never extracted or executed. Installing this package has no observable installer-side effect beyond ~57MB of disk use and a broken require. The shape (binary payload smuggled as `main`, generic `utils-*` name, no documented purpose) is suspicious and worth a human look in case a future revision lands a working loader, but the current artifact is non-functional rather than actively harmful.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
